long-title: People find it tricky to do things like reading, counting looking after money.

content: People with lower skills
People from vulnerable groups like disabled people
Patients
Homeless people
Adult education organisations
Adult skills teachers
Local councils
Charities
Voluntary groups
Community centres
GP practices
Hospitals

content.long-title: What would help?
content: Teachers for speakers of other languages able to teach reading counting and money skills alongside English
Offer training courses to people

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Making sure staff are trained

content.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: Accredited training accessed Face to Face or online

content.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: People having better understanding of reading, writing, counting and money management
People being able to use health and social care systems
people able to understand more about their health

content.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: We will know how many people have been trained
People will feel healthier
people will be less stressed
people will manage their health conditions better
people will be happier with the services they receive
people will have fewer worries about money

content.long-title: What will have changed in 1 year?
content: Health and local authority / social care organisations will know about health literacy
Training courses will be in place
staff will have been trained

content.long-title: What do we think will happen in the longer term (3 years)? For example, increased health literacy, better disease control, improved health
content: *People will feel healthier
people will be less stressed
people will manage their health conditions better
people will be happier with the services they receive
people will have fewer worries about money.

Training staff in health and local authority organisations to teach relevant reading counting and money skills

content.choice-subtitle: Health and local authority systems can't be understood and used by people with low skills

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Staff training in consultation tools e.g. Teach back, Chunk & Check
Staff & administrator training in using readability tool
Actions at the level of organisation to ensure materials can be read and used by people with all levels of skills
Staff trained to help people use the health and benefits systems
Using different ways if giving people information like TVs in waiting rooms, online videos

content.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: Accredited training accessed Face to Face or online
Training seen as an essential part of work

content.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: patients and clients with better understanding of health and local authority and social services systems
people able to understand more about their health
increased patient and client satisfaction
greater trust between patients / clients and health and local authority organisations

content.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: people will manage their health conditions better
people will be happier with the services they receive

content.long-title: What will have changed in 1 year?
content: more organisations will be aware of the issues
more staff will have been trained
organisations will offer a wider range of ways of getting information like using TVs in waiting rooms

content.long-title: What do we think will happen in the longer term (3 years)? For example, increased health literacy, better disease control, improved health
content: People will be happier with the services they receive
people will be living healthier lives
people will be using health and social care systems better
People will feel healthier
people will have fewer money worries
people will be less stressed
people will make better use of appointments
fewer people will not turn up to their appointments
